title: Level 633
wikitext:
{{DISPLAYTITLE:Level 633: "Snowy Town"}}{{Rewrite|date=June 9, 2026|1=wasted potential, resulting in 2/3rd's of the level feeling subpar. The outside is quite generic considering how many frigid levels there already are, Nothing regarding how or why the town got ruined is explained, much less the presence of Phenomenon 15 in the ruined town, resulting in the Frozen Ruins feeling random and without purpose. The last part of the level also doesn't have any explanation. If you are a mod, feel free to revert this or replace this with another template. also the sub-level (continuation of this level) of this is taking a while -it's taking a while cuz the author is currently blocked babes do we replace it with a rewrite template bc lightseeker is permabanned now}} {{Glacier Theme}} {{Background|file=633-4.jpg}} {{Notice|body=This page's vibes fit the level's theme '''much''' better in dark mode.}} {{Class Habitable}} Level 633, also known as "Snowy Town", is the 634th Level of the Backrooms. == Description == {{Blurimage|file=633-1.jpg|caption=The first image taken of Level 633.}} Level 633, also known as "Snowy Town", is a small, cozy town in the midst of a mountain-surrounded forest during a snowfall. The level is split into two sections. The first section resembles a well-lit downtown area. A snow-covered road winds through the town, traveling in a long, twisting, winding loop around and through the town with occasional small side roads branching off, but never traveling very far before dead-ending. The exception is a large branch-off that leads to the center of town and to the Outside of the town, causing the main road's general shape to be that of an enormous, distorted power button icon. The town is well-lit, having many decorations out that resemble Frontrooms holiday decorations, such as Christmas lights and wreaths. The town is quiet, the only sounds being those of the wanderer's own footsteps, and (depending on where the wanderer is) quiet, calming music coming from certain stores, often either instrumental Christmas music or piano ambience. The temperature in the town is a steady 25°F (-4°C). The weather shifts irregularly (every 4-16 hours) from light-moderate snow to still. The sky is perpetually overcast. The level has an 8:16 "day/night cycle" that consists of the sky being dimly lit for about 8 hours (as seen in the photo) and completely dark for 16 hours. The town appears to be surrounded by mountains on one side and a forest on the other. These areas are the second section of Level 633. {{Blurimage|align=left|file=633-2.jpg|caption=The buildings at the end of a side road.}} The shops to be found on the sides of the road perform a variety of functions. There are clothing shops, restaurants, cafes, hotels, bakeries, antique shops, toy shops, music shops, pet stores, laundromats, book shops, jewelry stores, game shops, hardware stores, furniture stores, garden centers, libraries, churches, hand-craft shops, and various other small, local-looking stores. There are no chains of any kind to be found in the town, although many of the shops do have names reminiscent of their Frontrooms counterparts, such as "Noble Garnets Booksellers" or "Moondoe's Cafe". The shops do not appear to have anyone running them, despite the well-kept appearance of the shops. There does not appear to be anything preventing wanderers from helping themselves to the goods provided inside the shops. At the center of the town is a large, snow-covered park. The park has a wide variety of trees scattered through it, both deciduous and evergreen, and long, cleared paths that wind beneath them. The trees and the path are both lit up by Christmas lights, and so are the numerous streams that flow from a large hot spring found from the center of the park. These often form small pools of hot water in various locations throughout the parks. Wanderers who have entered this level report feeling a sense of coziness, peace and well-being as long as they are in the town, as well as a reluctance to leave. This has not been deemed particularly dangerous, as the town is well-stocked with food, clothing and shelter. Wanderers can be convinced to leave the level in case of an emergency, and some have crawled under the Christmas tree and left by accident. Furthermore, wanderers often become curious about the Outside over time, and leave the town to go exploring after 4-6 months. == Entities == This section of the level is devoid of entities. == Colonies and Outposts == === The Colony of Lights === * A group who settled in Level 633 about 8 months ago * ~120 members * Willing to trade * Strongly discourage entering the Outside, as those who do don't return * Accepting new members == Entrances and Exits == === Entrances === * No-clipping through rare, large Christmas trees in [[Level 9.01]] and [[Level 611]] is difficult, but if successful, will lead to this level. * Entering a rare house with Christmas lights and decor in [[Level 461]] leads here. * No-clipping in [[Level 632]]'s water treatment facility during snowy weather leads here. * Very rarely, in [[Level 249]], the temperature will start dropping and one will feel a draft. Wandering will cause the level to transition to a well-lit store in Level 633. === Exits === * No-clipping in the town can only be done intentionally. Doing so leads to [[Level 634]]. * In the center of the town park is an enormous Christmas tree with a hollow space under it. If one crawls under the tree into the hollow space, they will enter [[Level 941]] upon exiting the space. * Leaving town by climbing over the walls or using the main road leads to the Outside... ----















''The air is colder outside the town...''















== The Outside == {{Class 4e}}{{Blurimage|align=right|file=633-4.jpg|caption=The mountains of the Outside.}} The Outside is the second section of Level 633. The Outside is an evergreen forest surrounded by a seemingly endless range of mountains. The air here is much colder, ranging from 5°F (-15°C) down to -15°F (-26°C). The 8:16 day/night cycle is still present in the Outside, but there are no lights to provide illumination at night, making traversing the Outside much more dangerous. The weather in the Outside ranges from breezy to heavy snow to blizzard conditions. Occasionally, the Outside experiences very large hail or heavy ice storms as well. There are no resources in this section of the level. The sense of safety and well-being that was present in the Snowy Town is removed slowly upon entry into the Outside. Wanderers tend to enter the forest out of curiosity while the coziness effect is still intact, only to have it gradually fade as they venture deeper in. Wanderers often become lost and sometimes contract hypothermia. Adding to the hazards of the Outside is the fact that the forest is somewhat unstable, as the blizzard conditions have been known to occasionally fell large trees. Clearings in the forest should be avoided, as they tend to contain frozen, snow-covered ponds that collapse upon being stepped on, resulting in a swift death by hypothermia for the wanderer. The mountains from a ring around the location of the Snowy Town, and they begin around 20 miles away from the town. The temperatures are even lower on the mountains, sometimes dropping as low as -30°F (-35°C). The wind is constant on the mountains, ranging from 20 mph to 50 mph, with gusts often exceeding 70 mph. Additionally, the rock that forms the mountains is unstable around the edges, and it is not uncommon for cliffs to crumble at even mild disturbances. The drops involved at these cliffsides can range from 100 feet to over 2000 feet. == Entities == The Outside is devoid of entities. == Colonies and Outposts == Due to the inhospitable nature of the Outside, colonies and outposts cannot be established here. == Entrances and Exits == === Entrances === * The only entrance to the Outside is to leave the Snowy Town by using the main road out (which dead-ends after half a mile) or by climbing over the fence that surrounds the town. === Exits === * Very few wanderers can survive for long in the Outside. Therefore, if they did not get lost, most choose to eventually return to the coziness of the...















...















...















''...What happened to the town?''















== Frozen Ruins == {{Class 3e}} The Frozen Ruins are the "first" section of Level 633. They consist of a dilapidated, frozen, abandoned ghost town surrounded by an evergreen forest known as the Outside. A frozen, slick, snow-covered road winds through the town, traveling in a long, twisting, winding loop around and through the town with occasional small side roads branching off, but never traveling very far before dead-ending, often in cramped areas with collapsed structures. The exception is a large branch-off that leads to the center of town and to the Outside of the town, causing the main road's general shape to be that of an enormous, distorted power button icon. The sidewalks next to the roads are icy and slippery, and are often cracked in places. The buildings have many decorations out that resemble Frontrooms holiday decorations, such as Christmas lights and wreaths. However, none of the lights function, and the decorations are often covered in ice and torn up. The town is quiet, the only sounds being those of the wanderer's own footsteps, the howling of the cold wind, and (depending on where the wanderer is) the sounds of broken DVD or VHS players making distorted noises in stores as their batteries drain. The temperature in the town is usually a steady -10°F (-23°C), though it can drop to -20°F (-29°C) during blizzards. The weather shifts irregularly (every 8-10 hours) from still to heavy snow or high-speed winds, up to 40 mph. Occasionally, the town even experiences blizzards or ice storms. The sky is perpetually overcast. The shops to be found on the sides of the road appear to have once performed a variety of functions. There are clothing shops, restaurants, cafes, hotels, bakeries, antique shops, toy shops, music shops, pet stores, laundromats, book shops, jewelry stores, game shops, hardware stores, furniture stores, garden centers, libraries, hand-craft shops, and various other small, local-looking stores. There are no chains of any kind to be found in the town, although many of the shops do have names reminiscent of their Frontrooms counterparts, such as "Noble Garnets Booksellers" or "Moondoe's Cafe". The shops are all abandoned and are no warmer on the inside than the weather is outside. Although items like jewelry, books and games can be found here, there does not appear to be anything of true value in these shops, aside from some frozen, half-torn clothing and the limited shelter the buildings provide from storms. If a wanderer is lucky, they may find canned food inside restaurants or cafes, or sealed foods that have been kept from going bad by their sealant and the cold. However, these are sometimes rotten and often stale. At the center of the town is a large park. The park has a wide variety of trees scattered through it, both deciduous and evergreen, and long, icy paths that wind beneath them. The trees and the path are both lined by Christmas lights, and so are the numerous streams that flow from a large spring found from the center of the park, though the lights are of course nonfunctional. The streams often form small pools in various locations throughout the parks. Many of these pools are frozen over and appear to be solid ground, but the ice on them is never thick, making them death traps for those unfortunate enough to walk across them. Wanderers who have entered this level report feeling a sense of confusion, disillusionment and tiredness as long as they are in the town, as well as a reluctance to leave. This has been deemed quite dangerous, as the town offers minimal food, clothing and shelter. Wanderers often wish they were back in the cozy version of the Snowy Town, and tend to search for the old locations they knew before they left to explore the outside. Wanderers always become exhausted and fall into despair over time, and they either succumb to hypothermia in the town or leave the town via the main road to wander they-know-not-where after 6-8 hours. == Entities == This entire level is devoid of entities. == Colonies == Due to the inhospitable nature of this section, as well as [[Phenomenon 15|certain phenomena]] present here, establishing colonies or outposts is impossible. == Entrances and Exits == === Entrances === * One can enter the Frozen Ruins from the Outside. === Exits === * One can return to the Outside by leaving the town. However, there are no exits to other levels. * One can also choose to follow the road and see where it leads you.















''You don't understand...'' ''It was such a cozy little village...'' ''What happened to it?''















''...''















''You stand up, a tear freezing on your face.'' ''You don't know why...'' ''But something about this cozy little town just...'' ''Being ruined like this...'' ''It seems so...'' ''...tragic.'' ''So wrong.''















''...''















''Somehow, you know that your chances of exiting this level are gone for good.''















''...''















''...you hate this cruel universe.''















''You stand up.'' ''You say your last goodbye to the town.'' ''And you set off walking down the dark road...'' ''Into the forest...'' ''Waiting for the inevitable to claim you.''















...















...















{{Collapse|initial='''> Open Author & Licensing Information'''|final=Error: Author Not Found (Level633.getAuthor() returned "Null")|body= One new file has been found. Opening. ''...'' ''Why did it have to be this way?'' ''...'' ''You take one last look behind you at the ruins of the town.'' ''You remember when it was a place of lights and beauty.'' ''A place where you and your friends could be happy.'' ''But you just had to go exploring.'' ''Now...it's all gone.'' ''And you'll never see your friends again.'' ''You're doomed to die in this frozen, lifeless wasteland.'' ''You turn around. The road stretches before you.'' ''You know it won't lead anywhere.'' ''You're tired. You don't want to walk.'' ... ''And yet you start walking anyway.''











{{Blurimage|align=center|file=darksnow.jpg|caption=...}}











''How long have you been trudging through this?'' ''...'' ''...You don't even know anymore...'' ''You may have been fortunate enough to leave during the "daytime"...'' ''But the light's not going to last much longer...''











''You're so cold...'' ''...'' ''That snowbank looks...'' ''...cozy...'' ''No. No, that's the onset of hypothermia speaking.'' ''...'' ''...Hypothermia...'' ''You pause to think about it.'' ''You could just...lie down there.'' ''You'd fall asleep...'' ''And that would be it.'' ''You'd be at peace.'' ''...'' ''This level isn't so bad, really.'' ''In fact, it's downright peaceful.'' ''...'' ''You lower yourself into the snowbank.'' ''You were right. It is cozy.'' ''Your eyes close for the last time, soothed by the faint light of sunrise...'' ''You feel the same way the old, cozy town made you feel...'' ''.........peaceful.........'' ''...''















''...''































''Wait...''















































''...?'' {{Blurimage|align=center|file=hopelight.jpg|caption=...Sunrise...?}} Author: [[User:Lightseeker12|Lightseeker]] }} [[Category:Safe]] [[Category:Cluster I]] [[Category:Levels]]
